content: Environmental health interventions operate at multiple levels: (1) Primary prevention—regulatory standards (EPA NAAQS for air quality, Safe Drinking Water Act), emission controls, chemical bans/restrictions (e.g., lead paint, asbestos), industrial permitting; (2) Exposure reduction—air filtration systems, water filters certified for specific contaminants, soil remediation, housing code enforcement, integrated pest management; (3) Clinical interventions—biomonitoring for lead/mercury levels, chelation therapy for heavy metal poisoning (controversial except for severe cases), asthma management plans addressing triggers; (4) Community advocacy—citizen science monitoring, environmental justice litigation, policy advocacy for stricter standards. Climate change mitigation co-benefits include reduced air pollution. Precautionary principle guides action when evidence is suggestive but not conclusive.
